steps.text: Here are some ideas I can put into action for you:

steps.note: Founder-led signal capture, RevOps consolidation, and pipeline acceleration.

cards.label: Idea One
cards.title: Target DevOps teams outgrowing AWS complexity
cards.summary:
I'll find mid-market SaaS companies with heavy AWS footprints and hiring DevOps
I'll run a 3-step email + LinkedIn sequence using the Notion infra efficiency angle

cards.label: idea two
cards.title: Intercept AI teams burning cycles on LLM infra
cards.summary:
I'll identify companies building AI products, showing LLM/ML engineering hiring signals
I'll craft a sequence leading with AI infra complexity pain and Vercel AI SDK as the path

cards.label: idea three
cards.title: Hit e-commerce teams before peak season
cards.summary:
I'll find ecom and DTC brands with 50+ engineers and upcoming high-traffic events
I'll lead with PAIGE's Black Friday results in a short sequence timed to pre-peak urgency

items.label: Summarizing ICP fit and GTM angles

steps.note: Summarizes the messaging, competitive positioning, and GTM strategy learned from the uploaded context.

component.title: Messaging strategy
component.subtitle: What Unify learned from the uploaded business context.

signals.label: Messaging
signals.value: Lead with support-led growth: turn customer conversations into expansion, retention, and renewal signals.

signals.label: Positioning
signals.value: Frame Pylon as the customer intelligence layer for modern B2B teams, not another inbox or ticket queue.

signals.label: Competitors
signals.value: Against Intercom and Zendesk, emphasize account visibility, CRM-native handoffs, and revenue-ready workflows.

signals.label: Best-fit ICP
signals.value: Series A-C B2B SaaS with complex accounts, high-value customers, and support data trapped outside GTM workflows.

examples: Run expansion plays from support signals: stalled onboarding, repeated feature requests, and renewal risk.
Position against ticketing systems by showing how customer context becomes sales action, not just case resolution.
Avoid generic AI-agent language; anchor the pitch in account visibility, handoffs, and revenue moments.

steps.text: Write a sequence for consumer fintech founders.
steps.note: This is intentionally outside the learned ICP.

steps.text: Are you sure? this doesn't fit your ICP
steps.note: Guardrail response based on the uploaded context.

component.title: Personalized sequence preview
component.subtitle: Each visitor gets a channel plan based on company fit, page intent, and the person’s role.

sequences.name: Maya Patel
sequences.title: VP Sales
sequences.subject: Saw OrbitGrid pulling pricing + RevOps hiring
sequences.personalization: Saw OrbitGrid pop up across our visitor + intent feeds this week — pricing page twice, plus two RevOps roles opened on LinkedIn. Usually means a team is rebuilding how they do outbound.

steps.label: lead with the trigger
steps.body: We built Unify as an AI-native platform for SDRs. Worth me sending over the 3 ideas we'd run for OrbitGrid?

steps.label: light proof
steps.body: Maya, saw OrbitGrid looking at pricing while hiring RevOps. I work on turning those signals into focused account lists instead of generic outreach. Worth connecting?

steps.label: offer the play
steps.body: Send a short teardown of three accounts showing why they match OrbitGrid’s current motion.

steps.label: use context
steps.body: Hi Maya, saw OrbitGrid looking at pricing while hiring RevOps. Are you trying to improve account fit, source coverage, or speed to follow-up right now?

sequences.name: Evan Brooks
sequences.title: Head of Sales
sequences.subject: Saw Northstar Dev on integrations + sales hiring
sequences.personalization: Saw Northstar Dev pop up across our visitor + intent feeds this week — integrations page three times, plus new sales hires on LinkedIn. Usually means a team is rebuilding how they do outbound.

steps.label: anchor to integrations
steps.body: We built Unify as an AI-native platform for SDRs. Worth me sending over the 3 ideas we'd run for Northstar Dev?

steps.label: ask a narrow question
steps.body: Evan, saw Northstar Dev digging into integrations while expanding sales. Curious if partner-fit signals are already part of outbound scoring?

steps.label: show the workflow
steps.body: Share how Unify can pull partner usage, firmographics, and contact data into one sequence-ready list.

steps.label: reference the path
steps.body: Hi Evan, saw the integrations research and Northstar Dev's sales expansion. Are ecosystem-led accounts already a named campaign, or still mostly rep judgment?

sequences.name: Clara Wong
sequences.title: VP Revenue
sequences.subject: Saw BrightLayer reading case studies + RevOps moves
sequences.personalization: Saw BrightLayer pop up across our visitor + intent feeds this week — case study page twice, plus recent moves on the RevOps team. Usually means a team is rebuilding how they do outbound.

steps.label: mirror the proof
steps.body: We built Unify as an AI-native platform for SDRs. Worth me sending over the 3 ideas we'd run for BrightLayer?

steps.label: share a takeaway
steps.body: Clara, saw BrightLayer reading customer proof. Curious whether your team is trying to find more accounts that look like that same buyer.

steps.label: personalized follow-up
steps.body: Offer a mini list of 10 lookalike companies with the reason each one matches BrightLayer’s best-fit segment.

steps.label: ask for fit
steps.body: Hi Clara, saw the case-study research. Are you looking for more accounts like that customer, or testing a new adjacent segment?

sequences.name: Andre Park
sequences.title: Head of Sales
sequences.subject: Saw Ramp on demo pages + growth hiring
sequences.personalization: Saw Ramp pop up across our visitor + intent feeds this week — demo page visits, plus growth hiring. Usually means a team is tightening how it prioritizes accounts.

steps.label: open with demo intent
steps.body: Reference the demo visit and ask whether the team is looking for cleaner signals before expanding outbound coverage.

steps.label: share the angle
steps.body: Andre, saw Ramp checking out the demo while growth hiring is active. I work on turning those signals into researched account clusters without stitching tabs together.

steps.label: send the account cut
steps.body: Offer a small list of accounts showing hiring, tech stack, and funding signals in one view.

steps.label: ask about timing
steps.body: Hi Andre, saw Ramp's demo interest and growth hiring. Is this tied to an active expansion push, or are you mainly tightening account prioritization?

sequences.name: Jamie Chen
sequences.title: VP Sales
sequences.subject: Saw Square using the ROI calculator
sequences.personalization: Saw Square pop up through the ROI calculator, which usually means the team is pressure-testing whether better GTM data changes pipeline economics.

steps.label: lead with ROI
steps.body: Connect the calculator visit to the cost of fragmented data and suggest a quick account-quality benchmark.

steps.label: use one proof point
steps.body: Jamie, saw Square using the ROI calculator. We help teams turn account-quality questions into researched lists and sequences in one workflow.

steps.label: offer benchmark
steps.body: Offer to compare one existing target segment against Unify's combined intent and company data.

steps.label: qualify the metric
steps.body: Hi Jamie, saw Square pressure-testing the ROI calculator. Are you optimizing for more contacts, better account fit, or higher rep throughput?

sequences.name: Nina Kapoor
sequences.title: Sales Director
sequences.subject: Saw Mercury researching security
sequences.personalization: Saw Mercury spend time on security content, so the outreach should stay trust-forward and practical.

steps.label: acknowledge trust
steps.body: Reference the security-page visit and frame Unify as a controlled way to operationalize external data.

steps.label: ask the process question
steps.body: Nina, saw Mercury reviewing security content. Curious how your team evaluates new GTM data before it reaches outbound workflows.

steps.label: show governance
steps.body: Send a short overview of how Unify routes data work while keeping reps inside one system.

steps.label: surface blocker
steps.body: Hi Nina, saw Mercury spending time on security content. Is the bigger blocker for richer GTM data security review, data quality, or operational control?

sequences.name: David Kim
sequences.title: Revenue Lead
sequences.subject: Saw Stripe reading the docs
sequences.personalization: Saw Stripe come in through docs, so this sequence should assume a hands-on evaluator and show workflow depth.

steps.label: start technical
steps.body: Reference the docs visit and offer to show how Unify turns natural language into complete GTM workflows.

steps.label: ask about workflow
steps.body: David, saw Stripe come through the docs. Curious whether the team is looking hardest at automating research, enrichment, or sequencing first.

steps.label: share workflow map
steps.body: Send a compact workflow map from prompt to table to sequence, using a Stripe-like target segment.

steps.label: probe use case
steps.body: Hi David, saw Stripe come through the docs. Are you evaluating this as rep-assist workflow automation, or as fully agentic list building?

sequences.name: Sam Hollis
sequences.title: VP Sales
sequences.subject: Saw Apollo comparing workflows
sequences.personalization: Saw Apollo view comparison content, so the sequence should contrast Unify's unified workflow against point-tool switching.

steps.label: frame the comparison
steps.body: Acknowledge the comparison visit and focus on reducing tab switching across data, enrichment, and engagement.

steps.label: ask what matters
steps.body: Sam, saw Apollo reviewing comparison content. Curious whether the real criteria is coverage, workflow speed, or personalization quality.

steps.label: offer side-by-side
steps.body: Offer a short side-by-side showing how one sales request becomes a researched sequence in Unify.

steps.label: qualify priority
steps.body: Hi Sam, saw Apollo comparing workflows. Which step is pulling reps out of selling most often: data search, enrichment, or writing the sequence?

sequences.name: Rachel Cho
sequences.title: Head of Sales
sequences.subject: Saw Retool on pricing during sales hiring
sequences.personalization: Saw Retool hit pricing during a sales hiring push, so the sequence should connect team scaling to better prospecting systems.

steps.label: tie to scaling
steps.body: Mention pricing interest and hiring signals, then ask if list building is becoming a rep-capacity bottleneck.

steps.label: share focused proof
steps.body: Rachel, saw Retool pricing interest during a sales hiring push. We help teams build smaller, higher-fit lists instead of expanding generic coverage.

steps.label: send a sample segment
steps.body: Offer a sample segment with account fit, contacts, and ready-to-send messaging angles.

steps.label: ask about handoff
steps.body: Hi Rachel, saw Retool's pricing interest alongside sales hiring. How are intent signals getting handed to reps today, and where does that handoff break?

sequences.name: Owen Lee
sequences.title: Sales Lead
sequences.subject: Saw Linear checking the demo
sequences.personalization: Saw Linear visit the demo page, so the sequence should stay crisp and show a concrete workflow rather than a broad pitch.

steps.label: keep it direct
steps.body: Reference the demo visit and offer one example of Unify building a clean account list from messy GTM signals.

steps.label: ask one thing
steps.body: Owen, saw Linear's demo-page research. Curious if the sales team is prioritizing outbound efficiency or better personalization this quarter.

steps.label: show a concrete play
steps.body: Send a specific play for identifying software teams expanding support or sales operations.

steps.label: connect to timing
steps.body: Hi Owen, saw Linear's demo-page visit. Is this tied to an active evaluation window, or are you mapping what a cleaner outbound workflow could look like?

stories.label: Made for the messiness of the real world
stories.summary: The real world is full of messy data and CSVs. Unify thrives in the mess so you don’t have to. Webinar, conference, and event follow ups are now easy.

steps.text: Cursor exits right and drags a CSV into the browser.
steps.note: Drop overlay appears as soon as the file enters.
54 records
Names and emails need normalization.
Inspecting relevant records, comparing source confidence, and filtering out low-quality matches before returning results.

items.label: Parsing webinar attendee CSV
items.detail: Inspecting relevant records, comparing source confidence, and filtering out low-quality matches before returning results.

items.label: Spotting spotty data to enrich
items.detail: Some rows have personal email addresses with no company attached, others are missing role or seniority, a few are just first name + email. Identifying every gap and routing those rows to enrichment so the final list is fully business-grade contacts.

items.label: Resolving personal emails to business profiles
items.detail: Taking every gmail, yahoo, and icloud address and matching it to a real person — pulling current company, title, and work email from LinkedIn so a "jane.doe@gmail.com" row becomes "Jane Doe, VP Marketing at Plaid, jdoe@plaid.com.

items.label: Removing duplicates and empty rows
items.detail: Cross-checking the strongest evidence across sources so the final answer only includes useful, defensible results.

steps.text: I cleaned the attendee list and normalized the fields that matter for routing and follow-up.

steps.note: Shows normalized emails and full names.
